license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wcjfaun
title: 0.1% Cresyl Violet Stain (pH 4.3)
author: Shaina Robbins

steps_raw.instruction: Measure 500 mL of ddH2O or molecular grade H2O and pour into a glass beaker

Aliquot 0.5 g of cresyl violet acetate into the glass beaker from the above step Cresyl Violet acetate pure high purity biological stain Contributed by users Catalog #AC229630050 Causes skin irritation May cause respiratory irritation Causes serious eye irritation

Put a magnetic stir bar into the glass beaker (containing H2O and cresyl violet acetate) and place on a hot plate at 100 °C and stir until cresyl violet is dissolved Allow solution to come to room temperature once cresyl violet is dissolved
Test the pH of the solution. Add glacial acetic acid dropwise until the desired (pH 4.3) is achieved Acetic Acid Glacial Contributed by users Catalog #A38-212
Pour the contents of the 0.1% Cresyl Violet solution through a sterile membrane filter into a light sensitive jar and close lid
